rationale: In the conceptual framework, the citing paper gives Dallas and Morwitz (2018) a dedicated discussion as the key prior study that compares low versus no effort in offers. It summarizes that paper's pseudo-free findings and then explicitly positions its own theory, tasks, and findings against it (discount purchases, more trivial token tasks, and higher redemption versus no-effort discounts). This citation is therefore used to motivate the research gap and define how the new contribution departs from prior evidence, not just as a background list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Dallas and Morwitz (2018) as the closest precedent on nonmonetary redemption costs, then extends the line to price-discount redemption with token tasks and a return-on-effort mechanism. It reports a different outcome pattern in this new setting, where token effort can outperform no-effort straight discounts.
